VCs: this job is investing in outliers. Also VC: here’s our revolutionary sourcing algorithm, custom built by our in house team, RLed on decades of data, with proprietary data signals from the moment someone is born. This job is very weird where there is so much serendipity and luck involved in how you stumbled upon an investment. However, it does take a lot of conviction and if you have years of bets where you have been right, then you aren’t simply “lucky”. Returns are the only alpha.. but those take time and so we tend to resort to random metrics to prove how we’re different from the next VC. The unscientific way I have tried to play this game is by massively expanding my surface of luck to try to find these outliers generally and having a prepared mind for 3-4 areas that are 6-12 months early where I can meet every entrepreneur. Time will only tell if this works!

"People assume prototyping requires writing code, but Image Gen is simply much faster." Rohan (Codex PM) showed me exactly how he does it: "I took a screenshot of the Codex composer where you can type in a new message. Then I asked Codex to use Image Gen to prototype 4-5 ideas to improve project selection. I can then ask it to take the first image and make a prototype of it on a Codex site. We usually think of Image Gen as useful for things like turning my hair blue. But it can create amazing digital mocks too." 📌 Full episode: https://t.co/jPa0m1bmCE
